div.head: Municipal Council of Dorchester
div.p:
Hon. Charles Francis Adams,
United States Minister at the Court of St. James:

Sir: We, the mayor, aldermen, and burgesses of the borough of Dorchester, in the county of Dorset, in council assembled, beg to approach your excellency with an expression of our deepest indignation and horror at the recent murder of the President of the United States of America, and the attempted assassination of Mr. Secretary Seward under circumstances of unheard of atrocity, and we desire through you to offer to Mrs. Lincoln our respectful and heartfelt condolence under her sad and awful bereavement; to Mr. Seward our sincere congratulations
at his providential escape; and to him and the American people our genuine sympathy with them at the loss of a ruler of whom Americans were so justly proud.
div.closer:
Given under the common seal of the borough of Dorchester the 2d day of May, 1865.
[seal.] JOHN PETTY ALDRIDGEHED, Mayor.
